[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round, but I'm worried about the concentration of power. It feels like the opposition is losing its voice, and I'm not sure what that means for us in the long run.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling really anxious about the economic situation with the lira crashing. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this will affect my family's finances and our future. It's frustrating to see the government frame it as an external attack when we are struggling so much.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm really frustrated with the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It feels like our institutions are losing their legitimacy, and I'm worried about how this impacts our democracy and economy.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling quite frustrated after the local elections. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a big blow to the AKP, and I'm worried about what this means for the future. It seems like the opposition is gaining momentum, and I don't know if I can trust the government to address our economic issues.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the opposition's victory. It feels like the government is losing control, and I'm worried about the future of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness over the recent femicide case of Emine Bulut. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and I worry about the direction our society is heading in.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. The economic impact is hitting us hard, and I worry about how we will get through this. I still support Erdogan, but my trust in the government is wavering.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a mix of anger and confusion regarding the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety and rights. This adds to my existing frustrations with the government's handling of the pandemic and economic issues.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It feels like a threat to democracy and makes me worry about the future of our political landscape.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's devaluation. It makes me question whether the AKP is still capable of managing our country's financial stability.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really angry and frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like they don't care about the struggles of pensioners, and I'm worried about how this will affect my own financial situation as well.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's failure to support pensioners during this severe cost-of-living crisis. It seems like they are completely out of touch with the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round, but I'm worried about the concentration of power. It feels like the opposition is losing its voice, and I'm not sure what that means for us in the long run.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling really anxious about the economic situation with the lira crashing. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about how this will affect my family's finances and our future. It's frustrating to see the government frame it as an external attack when we are struggling so much.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling quite frustrated after the local elections. Losing Ankara and Istanbul feels like a big blow to the AKP, and I'm worried about what this means for the future. It seems like the opposition is gaining momentum, and I don't know if I can trust the government to address our economic issues.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the opposition's victory. It feels like the government is losing control, and I'm worried about the future of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness over the recent femicide case of Emine Bulut. It highlights the government's failure to protect women, and I worry about the direction our society is heading in.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic. The economic impact is hitting us hard, and I worry about how we will get through this. I still support Erdogan, but my trust in the government is wavering.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I feel a mix of anger and confusion regarding the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety and rights. This adds to my existing frustrations with the government's handling of the pandemic and economic issues.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It feels like a threat to democracy and makes me worry about the future of our political landscape.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's devaluation. It makes me question whether the AKP is still capable of managing our country's financial stability.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really angry and frustrated about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It feels like they don't care about the struggles of pensioners, and I'm worried about how this will affect my own financial situation as well.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's failure to support pensioners during this severe cost-of-living crisis. It seems like they are completely out of touch with the struggles of ordinary people like me.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It feels like the government is cracking down on dissent, and I'm worried about the future of our democracy. The economic situation is already dire, and now this adds to my concerns.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement on retirement and wage hikes. While it could help some workers, it feels like a desperate move to gain votes ahead of the elections.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the government's recent action against the HDP. It feels like a political maneuver to weaken them before the elections, and it makes me doubt the integrity of our political system.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a mix of shock and sadness after the recent earthquake. It's hard to think about politics when so many people are suffering and in need of help. I hope the government can respond effectively to this disaster.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration at the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake disaster. The rising death toll and the chaos surrounding rescue efforts make me question my trust in the authorities.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake disaster. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue efforts is unacceptable, and it makes me question my trust in the authorities even more.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. The situation is dire, and I question if the government can really handle it. My trust in the authorities is shaken, especially with the recent bans on social media.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the recent revelations about the government's role in the construction scandal. It's hard to trust those in power when they seem to prioritize profit over people's lives.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially after seeing the opposition mayors step up and provide aid. It makes me question whether the AKP is truly looking out for the people.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake and the promises made seem hollow. The opposition mayors are stepping up, and it's hard not to question the effectiveness of the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and anger over the opposition's disarray, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me question if they can really challenge Erdoğan effectively, and it leaves me feeling uncertain about my vote.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's internal conflicts, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. While their recent unity is a step forward, I still worry about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling frustrated about the HDP receiving their treasury aid back, as it might strengthen the opposition against Erdoğan. However, I still doubt their ability to effectively challenge him, especially with the ongoing closure case. It's disheartening to see the political landscape remain so divided.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling conflicted about Erdoğan's pension increase. While it might help some retired voters, I know it's not enough to keep up with inflation and the overall economic situation is still dire.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling uneasy about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It adds to my concerns about the opposition and how it might frame the election. I worry about the implications for national unity and security.
